chief_complaint: A 10-year-old girl presented with high-grade fever, right hemiplegia, and left abducens nerve palsy.
history: A 10-year-old girl was diagnosed with acute myeloid leukemia (AML) and underwent intensive chemotherapy. She achieved complete remission after the first induction therapy but suffered from high-grade fever and neurological symptoms during recovery from bone marrow suppression. Cryptococcosis was diagnosed by detecting Cryptococcus neoformans in cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) analysis.
exam: Vital signs: high-grade fever. Physical examination: right hemiplegia and left abducens nerve palsy.
labs: MRI revealed two loci of T1 low/T2 high intensity in the brain stem area. CSF analysis showed detection of Cryptococcus neoformans by Biofire(R) FilmArray(R) meningitis/encephalitis (ME) panel. Blood tests: neutropenia.
correct_diagnosis: Central nervous system cryptococcosis with brain stem hemorrhage
differentials: Central nervous system cryptococcosis with brain stem hemorrhage
Meningitis due to other fungal pathogens
correct_ranking: Central nervous system cryptococcosis with brain stem hemorrhage
Meningitis due to other fungal pathogens
